steps.title: Identify what the data shows
steps.content: The simulation without bark water predicted decreased lichen survival during summer due to evaporative water loss; the control simulation with bark water did not show that decrease. This means bark water availability changes seasonal survival outcomes for at least some lichen species. The correct answer must accurately describe this mechanism without overstating it.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'only some specialized lichen species are able to absorb bark water during long periods of high evaporation.': Mischaracterizes the mechanism — the data is about whether bark water offsets water loss, not about which species can absorb it. ✗
  • Choice B — 'access to bark water may help offset the effects of seasonal water loss for some lichen species.': Accurately captures the simulation result: bark water availability corresponded with better survival during high-evaporation seasons for certain species. ✓
  • Choice C — 'although lichens can absorb water from multiple sources, most of the water they absorb is bark water.': Overstates the data — the study does not establish that bark water is the majority water source. ✗
  • Choice D — 'bark water absorption reduces biomass production in lichens but makes them more resistant to seasonal changes in conditions.': Contradicts the data — the simulations do not indicate bark water reduces biomass. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
